author: anon-13168fa2
content:
Delivered: Created 03-real-world-skills/04-shipping-it.md. Two major sections:

TESTING (~75% of chapter):
- Unit tests: what they are, when to use, what they catch, how to direct agent
- Integration tests: testing module composition, temporary test databases
- Smoke tests: the 'does it even run' baseline
- Property-based tests: proptest, random input generation, shrinking, what they find that hand-written tests miss
- Formal verification: Kani, mathematical proof vs testing, when it matters
- The tautological test problem: agents writing tests that mirror implementation instead of checking against spec, with before/after examples and specific prompts to fix it
- Finding edge cases: systematic categories (empty inputs, boundaries, special chars, concurrency, state transitions, resource limits)
- Cross-platform testing: VMs, WSL, GitHub Actions for multi-platform CI

DELIVERY (~25% of chapter):
- Clone and build (simplest)
- GitHub releases with binaries
- Cross-compilation via CI
- cargo publish to crates.io
- The packaging checklist (clean checkout, tests, clippy, README, secrets exclusion, fresh machine)

5 exercises. Updated README to link chapter. All Phase 3 chapters are now complete.
